name: Endpoint Unavailability
description: A GPU-backed Search endpoint is unhealthy during elevated traffic. The platform applies the same rolling-replacement pattern it uses at deploy time: stop routing to the bad pod, provision a fresh healthy pod, wait for readiness gates to pass, shift traffic to the replacement, then drain and terminate the unhealthy pod. The unhealthy pod is never restored — it is replaced. While the replacement is coming up, high-priority Search overflows to healthy or premium GPU capacity so the SLA holds during the gap.

traffic_windows.label: Healthy baseline
traffic_windows.event: normal Search traffic before readiness failure

traffic_windows.label: Readiness failure detected
traffic_windows.event: rolling replacement begins: bad Search pod removed from routing, new healthy pod provisioning

traffic_windows.label: Replacement capacity active
traffic_windows.event: replacement Search pods are healthy and serving; old unhealthy pod has drained and terminated

traffic_windows.label: Incident cleared
traffic_windows.event: rolling replacement complete; fleet is back to normal headcount on healthy pods

key_events.decision: Begin rolling replacement: stop routing to the unhealthy pod and provision a fresh healthy pod
key_events.evidence: Readiness checks fail on one Search endpoint while the remaining A10 pod is already above headroom.
key_events.outcome: Routing exclusion is immediate so no request lands on the bad pod; the autoscaler signal `replace_unhealthy_replicas` triggers provisioning of a replacement on the same instance class.

key_events.decision: Bridge the gap: high-priority Search overflows to healthy/premium capacity while the replacement comes up
key_events.evidence: Effective fixed-fleet safe capacity is cut in half during elevated traffic; the replacement pod is not yet ready.
key_events.outcome: High-priority traffic shifts to the H100 (which has headroom) and the surviving A10; the serving decision trace explains the cost and routing change. SLA is preserved during the readiness gap.

key_events.decision: Replacement pod passes readiness; traffic shifts to it and the bad pod drains and terminates
key_events.evidence: New A10 pod completes readiness probes; the unhealthy pod has no in-flight requests after routing exclusion.
key_events.outcome: The fleet is back to healthy headcount via rolling replacement — same mechanism the platform uses for planned version changes, just triggered by failure detection rather than a deploy event. Platform rejects stay at zero throughout.
